whatsappAutoreply.title: WhatsApp auto-reply
whatsappAutoreply.intro: Selective replies on your personal number (connected to smrtTask, Coexistence mode). Default: silent — nobody gets an auto-reply unless a rule matches them. Nothing is sent until you turn on the master switch.

whatsappAutoreply.masterWarn: When ON, people matching a rule get an automatic reply from your personal number. Turn on only after you've set up and tested your rules.

router.title: Update
router.description: Write freely and smrtTask will decide whether to create a task, update an existing one, or add an update.
router.placeholder: Describe what to do — add a task, update an existing one ("T42 pushed to tomorrow"), mark done, or add subtasks.
router.unknownFallback: Couldn't figure out what to do. You can convert the input into a new task.

dailyReport.generatedToInbox: Report generated and sent to your inbox

settingsParameters.title: smrtTask Parameters
settingsParameters.subtitle: Tune classifier behaviour, project discovery, and WhatsApp sync. Leave blank to use the system default.
settingsParameters.dailyBudgetHint: Once your daily AI spend exceeds this amount, new-message processing stops and per-task AI buttons return an error. Default $10.
settingsParameters.modelHint: Default: claude-sonnet-4-6. Pick haiku for cheap+fast, opus for higher quality.
settingsParameters.ruleThresholdHint: Part 3 won't propose a rule below this confidence. Default 0.7.
settingsParameters.projectMatchThresholdHint: A task won't auto-link to a project below this confidence. Default 0.7.
settingsParameters.projectClusterThresholdHint: Part 4 won't suggest a new project below this confidence. Default 0.65.
settingsParameters.whatsappLookbackHint: How far back Part 2 scans each run. Default 48.

onboardingSetup.scanExplanation: The initial scan fetches all messages and events, classifies them with AI, and creates smart tasks automatically. This may take a few minutes.
onboardingSetup.futureEventsHint: Future events — up to {months} months ahead
onboardingSetup.driveSearchPlaceholder: Search folder or paste Drive URL...
onboardingSetup.driveEmptyHint: Leave empty to scan all files (last 3 months)
onboardingSetup.skipAddressesHint: Add a full email (office@maor.org) or a domain (newsletter.com). Messages matching these are skipped entirely.
onboardingSetup.savingMessages: Saving messages... Processing will continue in the background.
onboardingSetup.backgroundProcessing: The system will process messages in the background and create tasks automatically.
onboardingSetup.largeMailboxWarning: Large mailbox ({count} messages) — background processing may take a few hours.
onboardingSetup.gmailCategoriesHint: These Gmail categories will be filtered out before processing. Recommended defaults are pre-selected.
onboardingSetup.calendarSelectionHint: Only events from selected calendars will be processed.
onboardingSetup.calendarNotConnected: Connect Google Calendar to select calendars.
onboardingSetup.calendarLoadError: Could not load calendars. You can change this in Settings later.

onboardingWhatsapp.title: WhatsApp Connection
onboardingWhatsapp.description: Messages reach smrtTask directly from Meta via DualHook — real-time, no Google Sheet.
onboardingWhatsapp.step1: DualHook routes Meta's webhook straight to the smrtTask backend
onboardingWhatsapp.step2: Every incoming/outgoing message is captured instantly; audio is transcribed and images are OCR'd
onboardingWhatsapp.step3: AI analyzes the conversations and creates tasks
onboardingWhatsapp.dualhookStep2: Paste the webhook URL and Verify Token shown below, click Save Changes then Test Connection
onboardingWhatsapp.dualhookStep3: After saving, fill in the Phone Number ID and WABA ID from the same DualHook screen here
onboardingWhatsapp.phoneNumberIdHint: From DualHook → Account Details → Phone Number ID
onboardingWhatsapp.wabaIdHint: From DualHook → Account Details → WABA ID
onboardingWhatsapp.businessIdHint: From DualHook → Account Details → Business ID. Not needed to receive messages or fetch media — captured only for future account-management features.
onboardingWhatsapp.accessTokenHint: From DualHook → Account Details → Access Token. Stored encrypted in Supabase Vault. Required for audio transcription, image OCR, and document storage.
onboardingWhatsapp.appSecretHint: From Meta Business → App → Settings → Basic → App Secret. Used to verify HMAC on inbound webhooks from Meta.
onboardingWhatsapp.verifyTokenHint: Pick any random string. Paste it here AND into DualHook → Webhook Override → Verify Token. We use it to validate Meta's initial GET handshake.

onboardingOrg.title: Create your workspace
onboardingOrg.description: Tasks, projects, and contacts live inside a workspace. You can invite teammates later.
onboardingOrg.changeLaterHint: You can change this later from settings.
onboardingOrg.slugInvalid: Use only lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens

adminSecrets.title: Keys & Secrets
adminSecrets.description: Manage encrypted platform-wide config and per-WABA WhatsApp credentials. Secret values are stored in Supabase Vault.
adminSecrets.webhookUrlHint: Paste this into DualHook → Webhook Override. Every connected WABA uses the same URL.
adminSecrets.platformHint: Values set once, serving every tenant (Gemini, Meta API version).
adminSecrets.envReadOnlyDescription: These keys are managed as environment variables and shared with an external service. You can only see whether each is set — to change one, update it in the hosting environment (Vercel, etc.).
adminSecrets.envReadOnlyHint: Read-only — environment variables shared with an external service. Edit them in the hosting environment, not here.
adminSecrets.connectionsHint: Per-WABA Meta secrets. Use this to rotate tokens after one expires.
adminSecrets.rotateHint: Empty fields are left alone — only fields you fill in get updated.

smrtReach.title: Outreach
smrtReach.subtitle: Multi-channel campaigns — pick an audience from smrtCRM, a channel (WhatsApp/email), and send
smrtReach.deleteCampaignConfirm: Delete this campaign? This permanently removes the campaign and all its recipients, queue, tracking and logs. This cannot be undone.
smrtReach.sendersSubtitle: Manage your verified sender addresses. Add as many as you like — not locked to one.
smrtReach.regionSubtitle: English sends from one region and Hebrew from another — editable.
smrtReach.subject: Subject
smrtReach.htmlBodyHint: Write your email here. You can format the text and insert fields from the list.
smrtReach.recipientsCount: {count, plural, =0 {No recipients} one {1 recipient} other {# recipients}}
smrtReach.sendConfirm: Send this campaign to all recipients?
smrtReach.whatsappPending: WhatsApp campaigns send via smrtBot (bot + template). On-screen editing — coming soon.
smrtReach.testBatchHint: Send a first batch then pause for approval
smrtReach.templateParamsHint: JSON array of template parameter components per Meta's format (type, parameters, etc).
smrtReach.templateParamsInvalid: Invalid parameters — a JSON array is required
smrtReach.testTargetRequired: An email or phone is required for the test
smrtReach.testBatchSent: First batch sent ({sent}). Campaign paused for approval.
smrtReach.sendNowConfirm: Send to all recipients now? It goes out immediately — ignoring Shabbat, send-hours and any schedule.
smrtReach.sendNowNote: "Send now" sends immediately, ignoring Shabbat, send-hours, schedule and rate limit. If a test batch is set, only it is sent and the campaign pauses for review. To send while honoring those limits, set a time under "When to send" and click "Schedule send".
smrtReach.scheduleSendConfirm: Schedule this campaign? It will send at the set time, honoring send-hours and the Shabbat rule.
smrtReach.scheduleSendStarted: Campaign scheduled — {queued} queued. Will send at the set time.
smrtReach.providerHint: Campaign type: send via Amazon SES (high volume) or via connected Gmail accounts.
smrtReach.gmailAccountsConnected: Sends via the connected Gmail accounts: {accounts}. The daily limit is split across them automatically.
smrtReach.gmailNoAccounts: No Gmail account connected for this org. Connect a Google account in settings to send via Gmail.
smrtReach.gmassConfirm: Send the campaign to the GMass seed addresses? You'll get a link to the placement report (Inbox/Spam).
smrtReach.gmassSent: Sent to {sent} seed addresses. The GMass report is available via the link below.
smrtReach.selectBotForTemplates: Select a bot to load its approved templates.
smrtReach.noTemplatesFound: No approved templates found for this bot. Create one and submit for approval (see guide below).
smrtReach.templateParamsNeeded: This template needs {count} parameter(s) — enter them in the parameters JSON.
smrtReach.templateGuideTitle: How to add a new template?
smrtReach.templateGuide1: Open the WhatsApp template manager in Meta (link below).
smrtReach.templateGuide2: Click "Create template" and pick a category: Marketing / Utility / Authentication.
smrtReach.templateGuide3: Write the message; for variables use Meta's numbered placeholders in order (1, 2, 3...).
smrtReach.templateGuide4: Submit for approval — Meta usually approves within minutes to 24 hours.
smrtReach.templateGuide5: Once approved, come back here, click "Refresh templates" and pick it.
smrtReach.tzHourHint: e.g. 10 = each recipient gets it at 10:00 their local time

guide.title: smrtReach guide
guide.intro: smrtReach sends multi-channel outreach. Pick an audience from your smrtCRM contacts, choose a channel — WhatsApp, email, or both — and send, with open tracking and unsubscribe handling.
guide.point1: Audiences are built on smrtCRM tags and segments — unsubscribed contacts are filtered out automatically.
guide.point2: WhatsApp goes through smrtBot (which enforces opt-outs and rate limits), and email goes through Amazon SES.
guide.point3: Every recipient gets a one-click unsubscribe link, and the preference is saved back in smrtCRM.

smrtReach.settingsSubtitle: Fixed email-sending configuration — addresses, accounts and caps. These do not change between campaigns.
smrtReach.sesSubtitle: Verified addresses for sending via Amazon SES, with a daily cap per address.
smrtReach.gmailSubtitle: Connect independent Gmail accounts for sending, separate from the account linked to the system.
smrtReach.gmailConnectNote: If Google shows a 403 error screen ("you do not have access"), the sending account must be authorized on Google's consent screen. Add the Gmail address as a Test user in Google Cloud Console (OAuth consent screen) and try again.
smrtReach.gmailConnectError: Failed to connect the Gmail account
smrtReach.allocationSubtitle: Choose which addresses to send this campaign from, and how many from each.
smrtReach.allocationNoSenders: No sender addresses — add some in the mailing settings.
smrtReach.allocationNeedSender: Select at least one address to send from
smrtReach.allocationNeedCount: Enter a positive amount for each selected address

smrtCRM.addMerged: Contact merged with an existing record
smrtCRM.allMatchingSelected: All {count} matching the filter are selected
smrtCRM.tagsDesc: Labels for categorizing contacts. A contact can carry several tags, and some are created automatically by the bot.
smrtCRM.segmentsDesc: A smart group — a rule that updates itself (e.g. "everyone with tag X who also has an email"). Available as an audience in smrtReach.

guide.title: smrtCRM guide
guide.intro: smrtCRM is your organization's shared contact database. It ingests contacts from the bot, from CSV import and from manual entry — keeping every one tagged with the source it came from.
guide.point1: Each contact gets a tag for the project it came from — contacts from multiple sources get multiple tags.
guide.point2: The system detects duplicates by phone and email and merges them automatically, so you never get duplicate records.
guide.point3: Search, filter by tag or segment and import lists — and the segments built here are available to smrtReach for outreach.

smrtBot.title: Bots
smrtBot.subtitle: Manage your WhatsApp bots
smrtBot.emptyState: No bots yet — add one to get started.
smrtBot.slugHint: Lowercase letters, digits and hyphens — used in the webhook URL.
smrtBot.timezoneHint: The bot's timezone — for time calculations (current day in tracking, scheduled messages, reminders).
smrtBot.adminPhonesHint: Admin phone numbers, comma-separated (international format, no +) — for notifications and admin commands.
smrtBot.appSecretVaultHint: The App Secret is stored encrypted in Vault — enter it once and it's saved for good; never shown again. Live and test can be different apps with different secrets.
smrtBot.appSecretHint: From the Meta developer dashboard — Settings → Basic. Used to verify the webhook signature; without it inbound messages are not processed.
smrtBot.callbackHint: Paste this in Meta under WhatsApp → Configuration → Webhook (Callback URL). One URL serves both live and test — the environment is detected automatically from the Phone number ID.
smrtBot.callbackVerifyNote: The Verify token you enter in Meta = the same value you put in the "Verify token" field below (a string you make up).
smrtBot.testHint: Simulates Meta's webhook verification. Save first.
smrtBot.testOk: ✅ The webhook answers verification correctly — Callback URL and Verify token are valid. Enter the same values in Meta.
smrtBot.testFail: ❌ Verification failed. Make sure the Verify token is saved and matches, and the bot is in Meta mode.
smrtBot.testNoToken: Enter and save a Verify token first.
smrtBot.hint_app_secret: From Meta — App → Settings → Basic → App Secret.
smrtBot.hint_phone_number_id: From Meta — App → WhatsApp → API Setup → Phone number ID.
smrtBot.hint_access_token: A permanent token from a System User in Business Settings (or the temporary API Setup token for testing).
smrtBot.hint_verify_token: A string you choose; enter it here and in Meta's Webhook config.
smrtBot.hint_phone_display: The number shown to users (display only), e.g. 972500000000.
smrtBot.menuWordsTitle: Words that open the main menu
smrtBot.menuWordsHint: Words that, when a user sends them, make the bot open the main menu — comma separated. Empty = default: תפריט, שלום, היי, הי, מה, menu, hi, hello.
smrtBot.menuDiagramHint: drag to pan · scroll to zoom · red = broken link
smrtBot.publishHint: Copies all test content (menu, messages, missions, trivia, holidays, FAQ, auto-messages) to live. The current live version is archived so you can roll back.
smrtBot.publishConfirm: Publish test content to live now?

guide.title: smrtBot guide
guide.intro: smrtBot runs your WhatsApp conversational bots — menu, game, FAQ and video — migrated into smrtesy.

smrtBot.waUnofficialWarning: The unofficial channel connects like WhatsApp Web and violates WhatsApp's Terms of Service — the number can be banned. Use a dedicated number, not your personal or main business line.
smrtBot.waScanHint: On your phone open WhatsApp → Settings → Linked devices → Link a device, and scan the code.
smrtBot.waNoGroups: No groups yet. Connect and sync to see the groups the bot is a member of.
smrtBot.waBroadcastIncomplete: Pick a group, body and time
smrtBot.webIntro: Turn on a web version of the bot and embed it on any site. The widget gives the exact same experience as WhatsApp — same menus, buttons and replies.
smrtBot.webEnableHint: Lets you embed the bot as a chat widget on any website.
smrtBot.webOriginsHint: One origin per line (e.g. https://example.com). Empty = any site allowed.
smrtBot.webEmbedHint: Paste this into your site HTML, right before the </body> tag.
smrtBot.webRegenerateConfirm: Generate a new key? The old embed code will stop working immediately.
smrtBot.webNotEnabled: Enable and save to get your embed code.
smrtBot.convSelect: Select a conversation to view it.
smrtBot.webIconHint: Image file: PNG, JPG, WebP or SVG. Square 512×512px with a transparent background recommended. Max 2MB (≤512KB recommended for fast loading).
smrtBot.webIconUploaded: Logo uploaded — click Save to apply.
smrtBot.webIconBadType: Unsupported file type. Use PNG, JPG, WebP or SVG.
smrtBot.transportMetaHint: The official Meta connection — requires an approved WhatsApp Business number.
smrtBot.transportBaileysHint: Connects like WhatsApp Web (a regular number, QR scan). Violates the Terms of Service — risk of a ban. Use a dedicated number.
smrtBot.waSwitchPrompt: This bot is on the official (Meta) transport. To use the unofficial channel (QR pairing, groups, broadcasts), switch it to the unofficial connection.
smrtBot.waSwitched: Bot switched to the unofficial connection.
smrtBot.waSwitchedMeta: Bot switched to the official connection.
smrtBot.aiHint: When a question has no FAQ match, the bot can answer with AI grounded in your knowledge base.
smrtBot.aiEnableHint: Off by default. Off = the bot hands off to a human.
smrtBot.menuSplitLabel: Split into separate messages (3 buttons each) instead of a list
smrtBot.menuEditOnTestOnly: Switch to test to edit (live is the published version).

folders.title: Folders
folders.subtitle: A folder groups the scripts of one production
folders.name: Folder name
folders.prefixHelp: 1–3 letters. Scripts are auto-numbered BR1, BR2, … and files named BR1_010.wav
folders.noScripts: No scripts yet — add the first one
folders.deleteConfirm: Delete this folder and ALL its scripts, lines and recordings? This is irreversible.

scripts.name: Name (optional)
scripts.tabHelp: The Hebrew tab is auto-selected; you can change it
scripts.pickFromDrive: Pick a document from Google Drive
scripts.searchFolders: Search folders or paste an ID / link…
scripts.noDocs: No Google Docs found in the folder
scripts.deleteConfirm: Delete this script and its lines? This is irreversible.
scripts.languageHint: Controls which pronunciation-lexicon entries apply: a Hebrew entry fires only on Hebrew scripts, an English entry only on English scripts. A Hebrew voice used in an English script gets the English pronunciations.
scripts.modelHint: The model this script renders with. Default inherits the model chosen in Settings; changing it here affects only this script.
scripts.emotionHint: Emotion processing for this script. Auto: off for chatterbox (fast, neutral), on for resemble-ultra. You can force it on or off.
scripts.parseFirst: Parse the script first to see its speakers
scripts.useBaselineHint: Adds the character's fixed style tags (slow/soft/…) to every line. Off by default — deep tag stacks can make the engine insert words or restart the sentence.

casting.title: Casting
casting.subtitle: Assign a voice to each speaker in this script
casting.speaker: Speaker
casting.noSpeakers: No speakers — parse the script first
casting.inheritedNote: Casting was pre-filled from the folder's first script; edit as needed

library.title: Voice library
library.subtitle: Every voice on the connected Resemble account
library.creditsNote: Credit balance isn't available via the API — check the dashboard
library.deleteConfirm: Delete this voice from Resemble? This cannot be undone.
library.generateMissing: Generate samples for voices without one
library.generateMissingConfirm: Generate samples for {count} voices that don't have one? Each one costs Resemble credits.
library.noMissing: Every voice already has a sample

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Manage voice generation projects
projects.deleteConfirm: Delete this project? This is irreversible and also removes its lines and recordings.

form.name: Project name
form.codeHelp: Used for file names and the Drive folder, e.g. BR1_010.wav
form.tabHelp: The Hebrew tab is auto-selected; you can change it
form.pickFromDrive: Pick a document from Google Drive
form.noDocs: No Google Docs found in the folder

characters.title: Characters
characters.subtitle: Manage voice clones
characters.deleteConfirm: Delete this character? It will be removed from your list.

form.styleBaselineHint: Tags applied to every line of this character — so each character keeps its own melody, even on neutral lines.
form.cloneHint: After creating — on the character screen you can upload a recording or pick files from Google Drive to create the (Ultra) clone.

settings.title: smrtVoice settings
settings.archiveFolderHelp: Where audio is saved when you "Save to Drive". Without it, saving fails.

guide.title: smrtVoice guide
guide.intro: smrtVoice turns Google Doc scripts into recorded audio in a cloned voice. This is the workflow from start to finished files.
guide.s1Body: Create a folder with a short prefix (e.g. BR). It groups all the scripts of one series, and every script gets an automatic number (BR1, BR2…).
guide.s2Body: Inside the folder, add a script: pick a Google Doc, the relevant tab, the language (Hebrew/English) and the mode — TTS (from text) or STS (from an editor recording).
guide.s3Body: Click "Parse" — the system reads the document and detects the speakers and line count.
guide.s4Body: Map each speaker to a voice: one of your cloned characters, a stock voice, or "skip" if it shouldn't be generated. You can preview a voice before generating.
guide.s5Body: Click "Generate audio". You'll see live progress — fetching, parsing, preprocessing and generating — line by line, until everything is ready.
guide.s6Body: Play a single line or all in sequence, download an individual file, mark problem lines for re-recording, and finally save everything to Google Drive.
guide.voicesBody: Voices are created and managed under Characters (clone from a recording or from Drive) and Library (every voice on the Resemble account). From there you assign them to speakers during casting.

cloneUploader.bgNote: Still training in the background — it will show as ready here shortly.
cloneUploader.voiceTypePro: Pro — high quality, ~few minutes of training
cloneUploader.successTraining: Clone is training (we'll notify you when ready)
cloneUploader.help: Upload a clean 30–60 second recording (no background noise, no music). Resemble learns the voice and returns a voice_id used for generation.
cloneUploader.progressTraining: Clone is training and upgrading to Ultra… ({status})
cloneUploader.driveFolderLabel: Drive folder link (with the recording files)
cloneUploader.noFiles: No audio files found in the folder

recordingUploader.help: WAV/MP3 file of the full recording. voice-engine will split it by silence and match each line to the matching segment.

studio.saveIncomplete: Save to Drive did not complete — please try again
studio.editHint: Edit the exact text sent to the engine. Phonetic respelling in Hebrew or Latin — never niqqud.
studio.learnedStat: worked for this voice · kept {chosen} of {total}
studio.reanalyzeHint: the LLM re-picks emotion + tone tags
studio.selectWordHint: Select a word in the text to get spelling suggestions
studio.takeNotePlaceholder: Which word to keep from this take?
studio.goodCount: {count, plural, one {good take} other {# good takes}}
studio.confirmDeleteLines: Permanently delete {count} line(s)? Includes all takes and files. Cannot be undone.

lexicon.title: Pronunciation lexicon
lexicon.hint: A phonetic respelling (Hebrew or Latin) substituted into the text before synthesis — not niqqud. How the word should sound.
lexicon.empty: No words in the lexicon yet
lexicon.deleteConfirm: Delete this word from the lexicon?

insights.title: What the system learned
insights.subtitle: Everything the system concluded from your picks — the spelling and punctuation that worked best, per voice. Star a take (⭐) and the preference is learned automatically.
insights.empty: Nothing learned yet. Star good takes (⭐) in the studio and the system will start learning which spelling and punctuation works best for each voice.

driveFolders.title: Drive folders to scan
driveFolders.description: smrtTask only scans folders listed here, including all their sub-folders. A folder that isn't on the list won't be ingested — even if you opened it recently.
driveFolders.empty: You haven't picked any folders yet. No Drive files will be scanned until you add at least one.
driveFolders.recursiveNote: Files anywhere inside a listed folder are scanned recursively. Changes take effect on the next sync.
driveFolders.driveNotConnected: Google Drive isn't connected. Connect Drive on the Account page before picking folders.

sms.title: SMS messages
sms.description: Connect your phone so smrtTask reads your text messages too and turns them into tasks. It uses the open-source SMS Gateway for Android app, which runs on your phone and forwards each incoming SMS here. One-time verification codes (OTP) are detected automatically and never sent to the AI.
sms.webhookReadyTitle: Your webhook URL (includes a secret token)
sms.webhookReadyHint: Copy it now — the token is shown only once. Register a webhook on your phone with this URL and the 'sms:received' event.
sms.empty: No device connected yet. SMS won't be ingested until you connect a phone.
sms.deviceIdPlaceholder: Copy from the app on your phone
sms.connected: Device connected. Copy the signing key and paste it into the app.
sms.errorInUse: This device ID is already registered to another account.
sms.step1: Install SMS Gateway for Android, turn on 'Local server' (ON), and grant the SMS read permission.
sms.step2: Open 'Connect device' here, paste the Device ID from the app's HOME screen, and click Connect.
sms.step3: Copy the Webhook URL shown here (the secret token is already embedded in it).
sms.step4: Register a webhook on your phone with this URL and the 'sms:received' event (via the app's API — the exact command is provided in chat).
sms.webhookLogHint: What your phone last delivered here — for connection diagnostics
sms.webhookLogEmpty: No webhook events yet (or messages arrived malformed / without a device id and can't be attributed). If you sent or received an SMS and nothing shows here, the phone may not be reaching the server (wrong URL, app asleep in the background, or no webhook registered for that event).

smrtPlan.title: Planning board
smrtPlan.lead: Every project, the timeline, and the dependencies between them · click a row for details
smrtPlan.liteNotice: View access — you can follow plans but not edit them

effort.desc: A one-off project with a deadline — a task list with dependencies and a schedule.
effort.example: e.g. a product launch, an event, building a tool

stream.desc: A repeating process — an episodes × stages table, every episode passes the same stations.
stream.example: e.g. a podcast, a weekly newsletter, a lesson series

roster.desc: A per-worker load view — gathers one person's tasks from every plan.
roster.example: e.g. how loaded the editor is next month

board.empty: No plans on the timeline yet
board.emptyHint: A plan is a project with a deadline (effort) or a process that repeats episode after episode (stream). Create your first plan to see it on the timeline.

matrix.title: Stream matrix
matrix.sub: A row per episode · a column per stage · click a cell to open the task
matrix.empty: No stages or episodes defined for this stream yet
matrix.emptyHint: An episode = one deliverable (e.g. a podcast episode). A stage = a station every episode passes through (transcribe, edit, publish).

pulse.title: Daily pulse
pulse.empty: No active focus commitments on this plan
pulse.legend: green = done · red = missed workday · gray = day off · dot = debrief filed

effort.empty: No tasks in this plan yet
effort.allDone: All tasks in this stage are done
effort.rosterNote: Aggregated view: tasks from other plans by assignee
effort.reopenReleased: Note — {n, plural, one {1 dependent task was already released} other {# dependent tasks were already released}}
effort.reblocked: {n, plural, =0 {nothing to re-block — all are in progress or done} one {1 task re-blocked} other {# tasks re-blocked}}

repository.title: Idea repository
repository.lead: Plans not yet on the timeline · each an idea doc that grows

guide.title: smrtPlan guide
guide.intro: smrtPlan is the planning layer over smrtTask. smrtTask answers "what to do now"; smrtPlan answers "is the year on track".
guide.s1Body: A plan is born in smrtPlan (vs a project, born in smrtTask). Effort = a task container; stream = an episode × stage matrix.
guide.s2Body: The engine schedules backwards from the deadline, marks the critical path, and releases blocked tasks when their inputs arrive — skipping Shabbat, yom tov and bein-hazmanim.
guide.s3Body: The engine updates status and surfaces risk, but never creates content or sends messages without approval. Humans win — every computation can be overridden manually.

edit.name: Name
edit.dailyMinutesHint: How many minutes a day to invest in this plan
edit.workdaysHint: The days a daily run is expected (default: Mon–Fri)
edit.editHint: Edit mode: drag a bar to move it, pull the edges to change date/length, click a title to rename, click an empty row to add a milestone, or add a new row.
edit.descriptionPlaceholder: What exactly to do — step by step, in plain language

gantt.connectHint: Click a task's link icon, then another task — to draw a dependency arrow.

templates.title: Plan templates
templates.hint: A template is a list of tasks (role + duration) plus a dependency chain. Applying it creates a new plan with the tasks, assignees and dependencies.
templates.name: Template name
templates.noItems: No tasks in this template yet

table.lead: Every task in every plan, in one place — click a cell to edit.
table.keyboardHint: Navigate: arrows between cells · Enter/F2 to edit · Enter moves down · Tab moves across · Esc cancels · Ctrl+Z undo
table.confirmDeleteProject: Delete this project and all its tasks and stages? This cannot be undone.

capacity.title: Team availability
capacity.hint: Each worker's work days and hours per day. Hours per day is what the engine uses to turn estimated hours into a duration in days.

estimates.title: Task hour estimates
estimates.hint: A catalog of task types and their estimated hours. The description helps the AI match the right task. Refined over time.
estimates.name: Task name

roles.title: Roles & staffing
roles.hint: A role is a discipline (designer, editor, tool-builder). Each role maps to one or more people; one is the default. A new task with a role is auto-assigned to that default — always overridable.
roles.name: Role name

my.title: My tasks
my.lead: Grouped by what you can do now · check ✓ when done
my.noTasks: You have no plan tasks yet

cell.title: Matrix cell
cell.noTask: No task linked to this cell

team.title: Tasks by worker
team.lead: Pick a worker to see all their tasks across plans — ready/blocked/done

smrtInfo.title: Info Center
smrtInfo.subtitle: All your important information in one place — ask in plain language and get an answer with its source.

ask.title: Ask your information
ask.placeholder: e.g. Who is FPL's insurer? When is Yehudit's life-insurance payment due?
ask.empty: No answer found in your knowledge base. Try rephrasing or add the info.
ask.vaultHint: The password is saved in smrtVault — open it and click 'reveal'.

facts.empty: No facts yet. Add one manually, or run extraction over your history.

field.required: Subject, attribute and value are required

profile.title: Context profile

smrtVault.title: Password Vault
smrtVault.subtitle: All your logins in one place, encrypted. Passwords themselves are never shown here — the browser extension autofills them on sites.
smrtVault.securityNote: Passwords are stored encrypted and are never shown on this screen. The smrtVault browser extension autofills them directly on websites.
smrtVault.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, username or URL…
smrtVault.empty: No logins in your vault yet. Add one or import a CSV from Chrome.

smrtVault.importNoPasswordColumn: No password column found in the file.

pwa.installBody: Add the app to your home screen for a fast, full-screen experience — no address bar.
